---
title: "Wetland_vars_correlations"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)

# -----------------------------
# 1) Read data
# -----------------------------
# Use your path; if you're running this in the ChatGPT sandbox, switch to the uploaded file:
# df <- read.csv("/mnt/data/daily_wetlands_dataset.csv")
df <- read.csv("C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data/daily_wetlands_dataset.csv")

# -----------------------------
# 2) Columns to standardize (by Site), preserving names
# -----------------------------
vars_to_scale <- c(
  # WL
  "WL_daily_mean","WL_daily_max","WL_daily_min","WL_daily_range",
  "WL_mean_2day","WL_mean_3day","WL_mean_7day",
  "WL_max_2day","WL_max_3day","WL_max_7day",
  "WL_min_2day","WL_min_3day","WL_min_7day",
  # GWT
  "GWT_mean","GWT_max","GWT_min","GWT_range",
  "GWT_mean_2day","GWT_mean_3day","GWT_mean_7day",
  "GWT_max_2day","GWT_max_3day","GWT_max_7day",
  "GWT_min_2day","GWT_min_3day","GWT_min_7day",
  # GWL
  "GWL_mean","GWL_max","GWL_min","GWL_range",
  "GWL_mean_2day","GWL_mean_3day","GWL_mean_7day",
  "GWL_max_2day","GWL_max_3day","GWL_max_7day",
  "GWL_min_2day","GWL_min_3day","GWL_min_7day",
  # Water area
  "Water_area_m2_3d"
)

# Keep only those that actually exist in the data to avoid errors
vars_to_scale <- intersect(vars_to_scale, names(df))

# Safe z-score function (NA-aware; if sd==0, return 0 for non-NA rows)
z_by_site <- function(x) {
  m <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  s <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.na(s) || s == 0) {
    # constant (or all NA) within site: keep NAs as NA, others as 0
    return(ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, 0))
  } else {
    return((x - m) / s)
  }
}

# -----------------------------
# 3) Standardize by Site (in place; names unchanged)
# -----------------------------
df_std <- df %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_to_scale), z_by_site)) %>%
  ungroup()

# -----------------------------
# 4) Model setup (same as yours)
# -----------------------------
# Define response variables
responses <- c("mean_WT", "max_WT", "range_WT")

# Define predictor groups
predictor_groups <- list(
  Solar    = c("Solar_daily", "Solar_2d", "Solar_3d", "Solar_7d"),
  WL_mean  = c("WL_daily_mean", "WL_mean_2day", "WL_mean_3day", "WL_mean_7day"),
  WL_max   = c("WL_daily_max", "WL_max_2day", "WL_max_3day", "WL_max_7day"),
  WL_min   = c("WL_daily_min", "WL_min_2day", "WL_min_3day", "WL_min_7day"),
  Air_tmean= c("Air_tmean", "Air_tmean_2day", "Air_tmean_3day", "Air_tmean_7day"),
  Air_tmax = c("Air_tmax", "Air_tmax_2day", "Air_tmax_3day", "Air_tmax_7day"),
  Air_tmin = c("Air_tmin", "Air_tmin_2day", "Air_tmin_3day", "Air_tmin_7day"),
  GWT_mean = c("GWT_mean", "GWT_mean_2day", "GWT_mean_3day", "GWT_mean_7day"),
  GWT_max  = c("GWT_max", "GWT_max_2day", "GWT_max_3day", "GWT_max_7day"),
  GWT_min  = c("GWT_min", "GWT_min_2day", "GWT_min_3day", "GWT_min_7day"),
  GWL_mean = c("GWL_mean", "GWL_mean_2day", "GWL_mean_3day", "GWL_mean_7day"),
  GWL_max  = c("GWL_max", "GWL_max_2day", "GWL_max_3day", "GWL_max_7day"),
  GWL_min  = c("GWL_min", "GWL_min_2day", "GWL_min_3day", "GWL_min_7day"),
  ppt      = c("ppt", "ppt_7day")
)

# Static predictors to keep for all models
static_predictors <- c(
  "Air_trange", "WL_daily_range",
  "GWT_range", "GWL_range", "Water_area_m2_3d",
  "Elev_m", "Size_km2"
)

# -----------------------------
# 5) Variable selection on standardized data
# -----------------------------
# Use df_std (standardized predictors) for the selection
find_best_predictors <- function(response_var) {
  # For each group, fit simple linear models with each member; pick best by adj. R^2
  results <- map_df(predictor_groups, function(vars) {
    existing <- intersect(vars, names(df_std))
    if (length(existing) == 0) {
      return(tibble(predictor = character(), adj.r.squared = numeric()))
    }
    models <- set_names(existing) %>%
      map(~ lm(reformulate(.x, response_var), data = df_std))
    metrics <- map_dfr(models, glance, .id = "predictor")
    best <- metrics %>% filter(adj.r.squared == max(adj.r.squared, na.rm = TRUE))
    best
  }, .id = "group")
  results
}

best_predictors_all <- map(responses, find_best_predictors)
names(best_predictors_all) <- responses

# Final predictor lists (best from each group + static)
combined_predictor_lists <- map(best_predictors_all, ~ unique(c(.x$predictor, static_predictors)))

# -----------------------------
# 6) Build model datasets (keep Site, Datetime, response)
# -----------------------------
model_data <- map2(combined_predictor_lists, responses, function(vars, response) {
  df_std %>%
    select(all_of(c("Site", "Datetime", response, vars))) %>%
    # ensure predictors exist (in case some static vars are absent)
    select(where(~ TRUE))
})

# Named outputs
mean_model_data  <- model_data$mean_WT
max_model_data   <- model_data$max_WT
range_model_data <- model_data$range_WT

# Optional: peek
# names(mean_model_data)
# names(max_model_data)
# names(range_model_data)

```


```{r}
# Load additional packages
library(caret)
library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)

# Set correlation threshold
cor_threshold <- 0.9

# Function to remove highly correlated predictors (excluding the response variable, Site, and Datetime)
remove_correlated_predictors <- function(df, response, threshold = cor_threshold) {
  # Identify columns to exclude from correlation check
  exclude_cols <- c(response, "Site", "Datetime")
  
  # Get predictor columns that are numeric
  predictors <- df %>%
    select(-all_of(exclude_cols)) %>%
    select(where(is.numeric))
  
  # Compute correlation matrix
  cor_matrix <- cor(predictors, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  
  # Find highly correlated variables
  high_corr <- findCorrelation(cor_matrix, cutoff = threshold, names = TRUE, exact = TRUE)
  
  # Drop the highly correlated variables
  cleaned_df <- df %>% select(-all_of(high_corr))
  
  return(cleaned_df)
}

# Apply to each model dataset
model_data_uncorrelated <- map2(model_data, responses, remove_correlated_predictors)

# Name the list elements
names(model_data_uncorrelated) <- responses

# You now have:
mean_model_final  <- model_data_uncorrelated$mean_WT
max_model_final   <- model_data_uncorrelated$max_WT
range_model_final <- model_data_uncorrelated$range_WT

# Optional: check which variables were removed
removed_vars <- map2(model_data, model_data_uncorrelated, function(orig, cleaned) {
  setdiff(names(orig), names(cleaned))
})
names(removed_vars) <- responses

# Check variable names
names(mean_model_final)
names(max_model_final)
names(range_model_final)

# Set the folder path
output_dir <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data"

# Save each dataframe
write.csv(mean_model_final,  file.path(output_dir, "mean_global_data.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(max_model_final,   file.path(output_dir, "max_global_data.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(range_model_final, file.path(output_dir, "range_global_data.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

*Getting summary statistics for each site*

```{r}
df <- read.csv("C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data/daily_wetlands_dataset.csv")

# Summarize by Site with means and standard deviations
site_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_meanWT   = mean(mean_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_meanWT     = sd(mean_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_maxWT    = mean(max_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_maxWT      = sd(max_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_minWT    = mean(min_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_minWT      = sd(min_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rangeWT  = mean(range_WT, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rangeWT    = sd(range_WT, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(site_summary)

output_dir <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data"
write.csv(site_summary, file.path(output_dir, "site_summary_table.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
